Main Question: What is ABC ID?
Alternate Questions: abc id kya hota; abc id kya rehta; abc id kaiku banana; why abc id; why should i create abc id; abc id kyun banaein;
Answer: An ABC ID (Academic Bank of Credits ID) is a unique 12-digit digital identification number for students in India that acts like a virtual bank account for academic credits. It securely stores, transfers, and redeems credits earned from different universities, allowing students to seamlessly change colleges, switch courses, or resume their education after a break. Students can easily generate this permanent digital ID for free using their Aadhaar card on government platforms like DigiLocker or the official abc.gov.in portal.

Main Question: How to create ABC ID?
Alternate Questions: abc id kaisa banana; create new ABC ID; create academic bank of credits ID
Answer:
ABC ID can be created using  either of the 2 following methods: 
1. ABC Portal -    https://www.abc.gov.in
2. DigiLocker -  https://www.digilocker.gov.in
You can find the complete guide for creating your ABC ID by clicking on the following link: https://www.caluniv-ucsta.net/ma/notice-2026/How%20to%20create%20ABC%20Id%20or%20APAAR%20Id.pdf

Main Question: Can I claim more than one reservation category?
Alternate Questions: multiple reservation; do categories; sc and pwd together; reservation combination
Answer:
Yes, students can claim more than one reservation through a system of horizontal reservation. There are twocategories of reservations. 
Vertical Reservations (Caste/Community): These are the primary categories like General, EWS, OBC, SC, and ST. You can only claim one of these. If you qualify under merit, you may be considered for the general unreserved pool without using your reserved quota.
Horizontal Reservations (Special Categories): These are additional quotas that cut across all vertical categories. Examples include Women, Persons with Disabilities (PwD), Children of Armed Personnel (CAP), NCC, or Sports quotas. 
For instance, if you are an OBC student who is also a female athlete with a Sports quota, you can simultaneously claim your OBC status along with the Women’s and Sports reservations. The seat allocation system generally prioritizes candidates who have the highest number of applicable claims alongside high merit.

Main Question: What is the eligibility criteria for B.Ed admission?
Alternate Questions: bed eligibility kya; b.ed admission qualification; b.ed ke liye graduation compulsory; b.ed admission rules
Answer:
The eligibility requirements for admission to the B.Ed. programme are as follows:
•The applicant must be a trained in-service teacher engaged in elementary education; and
•Must have completed an NCTE-recognized teacher education programme through the face-to-face mode; and
•Must have secured at least 50% marks either in the Bachelor's degree and/or the Master's degree in Sciences, Social Sciences, Commerce, or Humanities from a UGC-recognized University; or
•Must possess a Bachelor's degree in Engineering or Technology with specialization in Science and Mathematics with at least 55% marks, or any other equivalent qualification recognized by the University; and
•Must have studied Urdu either as a subject or as the medium of instruction up to the 10th standard or its equivalent.
Candidates must fulfil all the above eligibility conditions at the time of admission.
